code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Helper dictionaries for phrasing
    simple_phrases = {
        "birthDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born on {o}",
        "birthYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "deathDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} died on {o}",
        "deathPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} died in {o}",
        "birthPlace": lambda s, o: f"{s} was born in {o}",
        "origin": lambda s, o: f"{s} originates from {o}",
        "nationality": lambda s, o: f"{s} is from {o}",
        "occupation": lambda s, o: f"{s} works as {o}",
        # Remove the extra article for background to match expected phrasing
        "background": lambda s, o: f"{s} is {o.strip('\"')}",
        # Use a more natural expression for active start year
        "activeYearsStartYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} became active in {o}",
        "activeYearsEndYear": lambda s, o: f"{s} ended their career in {o}",
        # Phrase instrument in a way that works for genres as well
        "instrument": lambda s, o: f"The {o} is used in {s}",
        # Phrase genre to highlight the musical genre explicitly
        "genre": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s musical genre is {o}",
        "recordLabel": lambda s, o: f"{s} is signed to {o}",
        "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist": lambda s, o: f"{s} is associated with {o}",
        "bandMember": lambda s, o: f"{s} includes member {o}",
        "parentCompany": lambda s, o: f"{s} is owned by {o}",
        "location": lambda s, o: f"{s} is located in {o}",
        "leader": lambda s, o: f"{o} is the leader of {s}",
        "capital": lambda s, o: f"{s} has capital {o}",
        "currency": lambda s, o: f"{s} uses the {o}",
        "anthem": lambda s, o: f"{s}'s anthem is {o}",
        # Improved phrasing for country relations
        "country": lambda s, o: f"{s} is in the country of {o}",
        "isPartOf": lambda s, o: f"{s} is part of {o}",
        "derivative": lambda s, o: f"{o} is derived from {s}",
        "governingBody": lambda s, o: f"{s} is governed by {o}",
        "leaderTitle": lambda s, o: f"{s} has leader titled {o}",
        "language": lambda s, o: f"The {o} is spoken in {s}",
        "officialLanguage": lambda s, o: f"The official language of {s} is {o}",
        "ethnicGroup": lambda s, o: f"The main ethnic group of {s} is {o}",
        "populationDensity": lambda s, o: f"{s} has a population density of {o}",
        "meaning": lambda s, o: f"The meaning of {s} is {o}",
        "longName": lambda s, o: f"{s} is officially called {o}",
        "areaTotal": lambda s, o: f"{s} covers an area of {o}",
        "foundingDate": lambda s, o: f"{s} was founded on {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda s, o: f"{s} lies at {o} meters above sea level",
        "postalCode": lambda s, o: f"The postal code of {s} is {o}",
        "musicFusionGenre": lambda s, o: f"{o} is a musical fusion of {s}",
        "musicSubgenre": lambda s, o: f"{o} is a subgenre of {s}",
        # More natural phrasing for stylistic origins
        "stylisticOrigin": lambda s, o: f"{s} has its stylistic origins in {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject for coherent sentences
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_groups = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_groups[t.subject].append(t)

    sentences = []

    # Process each subject group
    for subject, group in subject_groups.items():
        # Separate genre‑fusion triples (where subject is a genre) from person/artist triples
        genre_fusion = [t for t in group if t.predicate in ("musicFusionGenre", "musicSubgenre", "stylisticOrigin")]
        other = [t for t in group if t.predicate not in ("musicFusionGenre", "musicSubgenre", "stylisticOrigin")]

        parts = []

        # Build description for the main subject (person/artist)
        # Aggregate predicates for the current subject
        from collections import defaultdict
        agg = defaultdict(list)   # predicate -> list of objects
        for t in other:
            agg[t.predicate].append(t.object)

        # Helper to join list with commas and "and"
        def join_list(items):
            if not items:
                return ""
            if len(items) == 1:
                return items[0]
            return ", ".join(items[:-1]) + f", and {items[-1]}"

        attr_phrases = []

        # Combine genre and associated bands into a natural phrase if both exist
        if "genre" in agg and "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist" in agg:
            genres = join_list(agg["genre"])
            bands = agg["associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist"]
            band_word = "band" if len(bands) == 1 else "bands"
            band_list = join_list(bands)
            attr_phrases.append(f"{subject} plays {genres} for the {band_word} {band_list}")
            del agg["genre"]
            del agg["associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist"]
        else:
            # If only associated bands without genre
            if "associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist" in agg:
                bands = agg["associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist"]
                band_word = "band" if len(bands) == 1 else "bands"
                band_list = join_list(bands)
                attr_phrases.append(f"{subject} is associated with the {band_word} {band_list}")
                del agg["associatedBand/associatedMusicalArtist"]

        # Process each aggregated predicate
        for pred, objects in agg.items():
            phrase_func = simple_phrases.get(pred)
            if pred == "origin":
                # Use a more natural phrasing for multiple origins
                location = join_list(objects)
                attr_phrases.append(f"{subject} is from {location}")
            elif pred == "nationality":
                nat = join_list(objects)
                attr_phrases.append(f"{subject} is {nat}")
            elif pred == "genre":
                # If multiple genres, list them
                if len(objects) == 1:
                    attr_phrases.append(phrase_func(subject, objects[0]))
                else:
                    genres = join_list(objects)
                    attr_phrases.append(f"{subject} performs {genres} music")
            elif pred == "instrument":
                instr = join_list(objects)
                attr_phrases.append(f"{subject} plays {instr}")
            elif pred == "background":
                # Strip possible surrounding quotes
                bg = objects[0].strip('\"')
                attr_phrases.append(f"{subject} is a {bg}")
            elif pred == "recordLabel":
                # Join multiple record labels naturally
                labels = join_list(objects)
                attr_phrases.append(f"{subject} is signed to {labels}")
            elif phrase_func:
                # For predicates with a single simple phrase, use the first object
                attr_phrases.append(phrase_func(subject, objects[0]))
            else:
                # Fallback generic wording
                attr_phrases.append(f"{subject} {pred} {join_list(objects)}")

        if attr_phrases:
            # Combine attributes with commas, using "and" before the last
            if len(attr_phrases) == 1:
                parts.append(attr_phrases[0])
            else:
                parts.append(", ".join(attr_phrases[:-1]) + f", and {attr_phrases[-1]}")

        # Add genre fusion information if present
        for t in genre_fusion:
            phrase_func = simple_phrases.get(t.predicate)
            if phrase_func:
                parts.append(phrase_func(t.subject, t.object))
            else:
                parts.append(f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {t.object}")

        if parts:
            sentences.append(" ".join(parts) + ".")

    # Combine all subject sentences into one paragraph
    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
